name: Jaegerschnitzel
recipeInstructions:
1. Get pork- or veal Schnitzels.
2. Wash and dry the meat, then rub with pepper, salt, and a small amount of brown sugar.
3. Cook in butter in an open pan on both sides until the meat is cooked through and has a nice brown color. Set aside and keep hot.
4. In the remaining meat juices in the pan, cook fresh mushrooms (about one pound for two Schnitzels; any type works, but white supermarket mushrooms or a mix of wild forest mushrooms are preferred) until most liquid evaporates. Season with pepper, salt, and additional brown sugar to taste.
5. Stir in unwhipped cream until the sauce thickens.
6. Pour the sauce over the Schnitzels and serve with French fries or croquettes.
7. Variations: Add a splash of cooking sherry, sautéed onions, and garlic to the mushroom gravy (onions and garlic cook with the mushrooms; sherry is added with the cream).
